data.title: Western Digital in focus as Citi trims price target but stays positive into spin-off
data.description:
• Western Digital (NASDAQ:WDC) was in focus on Tuesday as Citi trimmed its price target on the memory maker but reiterated its Buy rating ahead of the company's split of its flash memory and hard disk drive businesses, slated to occur sometime before the end of the year.
  • Shares rose fractionally in premarket trading.
  • "While we recognize NAND near-term fundamentals remain volatile, we continue to remain constructive on the forthcoming spin-off unlocking value," Citi analyst Asiya Merchant wrote in a note to clients.
  • Merchant, who lowered her price target to $85 from $87, added that at current levels, the hard disk drive accounts for roughly 95% of Western Digital's valuation, while the flash business is roughly 5%.

data.title: UBS favors buy-rated DECK, WWW, TJX, and ONON
data.description:
The UBS global research team came out on Tuesday and showcased that when scanning through its quantitative factor analysis four names popped up as buy-ratings. Those four names were Deckers Outdoor Corporation (NYSE:DECK), Wolverine World Wide (NYSE:WWW), TJX Companies (NYSE:TJX), and On Holding AG (NYSE:ONON).

UBS added that it believes DECK, WWW, TJX, and ONON each have solid fundamental outlooks. See below what the financial institution had to say about each of the four stocks:

DECK: “Hoka is one of the world's fastest growing footwear brands. We forecast it delivering a 21% 5-yr. sales CAGR and this is the main driver of our 15.5% 5-yr. EPS CAGR forecast for DECK. We believe this growth should drive stock outperformance.”

WWW: “WWW has streamlined its portfolio by divesting and licensing brands over the last 2 years, allowing it to re-invest in its business. We think its Active segment's return to growth and cost savings initiatives should drive a 25% 4-yr. EPS CAGR post FY24E.”

TJX: “We view TJX as capable of taking major market share from Department Store peers over the next few years. Plus, we believe TJX's newer businesses such as HomeSense and Sierra Trading Post have big potential, as does its international operation. We forecast TJX delivering a ~12.5% 5-yr. EPS CAGR.”

ONON: “We view On as one of the world's fastest growing athletic wear brands and worthy of a premium multiple. We forecast On delivering a 43% 5-year EPS CAGR and think its growth will surprise the market, driving stock outperformance.”

Year-to-date price action: DECK +81.2%, WWW +164.6%, TJX +34.5%, and ONON +117%.

data.title: Nerdy regains compliance with NYSE
data.description:
• Nerdy (NYSE:NRDY) said it has regained compliance with the New York Stock Exchange.
  • The company was not in compliance with the minimum share price requirement.

data.title: Scotiabank Q4 earnings top consensus, but revenue falls short
data.description:
The Bank of Nova Scotia (NYSE:BNS), known familiarly as Scotiabank, turned in better-than-expected earnings on Tuesday as its noninterest expenses dropped from a year ago, but revenue missed the mark, both in net interest income and noninterest income.

Fiscal Q4 EPS of C$1.57 (US$), topping the Visible Alpha consensus of C$1.60, fell from C$1.63 in Q3 and rose from C$1.23 in Q4 2023.

Net interest income for the quarter ended Oct. 31, 2024, increased to C$4.92B from C$4.86B in the previous quarter and C$4.67B in the year-ago period, but missed the Visible Alpha consensus of C$4.95B.

Noninterest income of C$3.60B, lagging the Visible Alpha estimate of C$3.69B, climbed from C$3.50B in Q3 and edged down from C$3.61B in last year's Q4.

Scotiabank's (NYSE:BNS) total revenue of C$8.53B grew from C$8.36B in the prior quarter and C$8.27B a year ago. The current quarter's revenue missed the Visible Alpha estimate of C$8.64B.

Scotiabank (BNS) ordinary shares fell 2.0% in premarket trading.

its provision for credit losses declined to C$1.03B from C$1.05B in Q3 and C$1.26B in Q4 2023.

Noninterest expenses of C$5.30B increased from C$4.95B in the previous quarter and dropped from C$5.53B a year ago.

Common equity tier 1 capital ratio of 13.1% slipped from 13.3% in Q3 and edged up from 13.0% in last year's Q4.

Average total net loans and acceptances of C$756B fell from C$761B in Q3. Average deposits of C$712B compared with C$711B in the prior quarter.

Canadian Banking adjusted net income of C$1.06B fell 4% Q/Q and climbed 34% Y/Y.

Global Wealth Management adjusted net income of C$426M rose 3% Q/Q and 28% Y/Y. Revenue climbed 13% Y/Y on higher mutual fund fees and brokerage revenue across Canadian and international wealth businesses.

Global Banking and Markets net income of C$403M dropped 4% Q/Q and 3% Y/Y. Business Banking revenue declined 4% Y/Y and Capital Markets revenue increased 7% Y/Y.

International Banking adjusted net income of C$634M dipped 4% Q/Q and increased 18% Y/Y.

Earlier, Bank of Nova Scotia non-GAAP EPS of C$1.57, revenue of C$8.53B

data.title: BlackRock AAA CLO ETF declares monthly distribution of $0.2605

  • Payable Dec 05; for shareholders of record Dec 02; ex-div Dec 02.

data.title: AURI to launch gold backed crypto coin
data.description:
• Auri (OTCPK:AURI), on Tuesday, announced a joint venture with Gold Diamond Wawa Property Trust for acquisition/development of six patented claims in McMurray Township, in Canada.
  • The properties are located near the Red Pine's Wawa gold project, where recent drilling results indicate resource estimates north of 3 million ounces of gold.
  • Auri will own and collateralize the Gold reserves, and use the added value for its AU, Auri Tokens, as a "Gold Backed" crypto coin. Auri crypto coins are listed on the Salavi global exchange.

data.title: Cunard cruise bookings up 23% from last year
data.description:
With the launch of its newest ship, the Queen Anne, and three new cruise itineraries, Cunard (NYSE:CCL) saw the highest number of bookings for the first 10 months of 2024, a 23% increase from the same period last year.

Between January 1 and October 31, the British cruise line saw a 49% increase in first-time guests, with more than half of its total bookings now coming from outside the UK.

“As we look ahead to 2025, we are proud to report record-breaking bookings that demonstrate the continued strength of Cunard's commercial performance,” said Cunard President, Katie McAlister.

Carnival (CCL) purchased a 68% stake in Cunard in 1998 and the remaining 32% a year later.

data.title: Nasdaq, S&P, Dow futures tread cautiously with labor data on tap
data.description:
Stock index futures were almost flat on Tuesday, as investors turn their attention to a key labor report.

S&P 500 futures (SPX) were unchanged, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) -0.1%, and Dow futures (INDU) were unchanged.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) fell 2 basis points to 4.17%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) fell 3 basis points to 4.16%.

Major market averages closed out Monday’s session in a mixed fashion as the financial community digested the latest comments from President-elect Donald Trump as he threatened to implement more trade tariffs.

"Markets actually put in a decent performance yesterday, with the S&P 500 moving up to yet another record high. One supportive factor was an upside surprise in the ISM manufacturing for November," Deutsche Bank's Jim Reid said.

That equity rally was led by further strength among big tech stocks, and the Nasdaq and Magnificent 7 moved up to record highs of their own, Reid added.

The October Job Openings and Labor Turnover Survey (JOLTS) report will land during market hours. It is one of the first of a few labor data expected in the week. Economists forecast a rise to 7.490M.

"The survey response rate is so low the numbers would not be published elsewhere. Recent variations in vacancies represented shifts between internal and external advertised positions," UBS' Paul Donovan said.

Vacancies may be in focus if President-elect Donald Trump pursues deportations that create labor market bottlenecks, Donovan added.

data.title: Zscaler slides on slow billings ramp but remains on track for growth
data.description:
Zscaler (NASDAQ:ZS) slipped following its first quarter 2025 earnings report, likely due to slower than anticipated billings acceleration, but investment firms remain confident in the security firm moving forward.

The company also announced the retirement of long-time CFO Remo Canessa.

"But 30% y/y bookings growth this quarter mitigates slightly lighter billings, in our view, giving us greater confidence the company can deliver better 2H billings growth needed to hit full-year guidance," said J.P. Morgan analysts, led by Brian Essex, in an investor note. "Additionally, our estimates of contracted versus unscheduled billings growth imply much less work is needed to deliver a second half billings acceleration in FY25 than some may assume."

J.P. Morgan retained its Overweight rating on the stock and a $240 12-month price target.

Bernstein Societe Generale said the pullback in share prices following the earnings results might be related to the second half of fiscal year 2025 being about $6M below consensus.

"Consensus is usually $1-2M above mid-point each quarter, so this might force sell-side models down a little," said Bernstein analysts, led by Peter Weed, in an investor note.

"Some good signals include the scheduled billings growth at 7%, while the unscheduled billings growth exceeded 20%, which points to sales reps performance driving growth," he added. "With the win rates remaining strong, customer churn at a normal level, and the deal sizes growing, the incremental QoQ growth momentum in the second half of FY2025 would seem to accelerate."

Morgan Stanley also eyes steep second half fiscal 2025 acceleration due to a sales team transition.

"Total billings grew 13%, above consensus at 10%, but a lighter beat than usual," said Morgan Stanley analysts, led by Hamza Fodderwala, in an investor note. "Management noted mechanical headwinds related to contracted 'scheduled billings' that are being invoiced from active contracts, but growing slower in 1H."

"As new reps continue to onboard, management expects productivity to ramp over the next year and be more back-half weighted given that it takes about one year to ramp to full productivity," he added.

Morgan Stanley maintained an Equal-weight rating on the stock and increased its price target to $215 from $205.

Zscaler competitors were fairly flat during early Tuesday trading, including Fortinet (FTNT) and Palo Alto Networks (PANW) moving less than 1% in either direction.

data.title: Hedge funds going long On Semi, short NXP, Wells Fargo says
data.description:
Research firm Wells Fargo has weighed in on the automotive and industrial-focused part of the semiconductor industry and the overarching view from investors appears to be mixed.

"Investors largely agree w/ our view the auto semi trade is in a slightly easier / better position than industrials given [inventory] digestion across both [markets]," analyst Joe Quatrochi wrote in a note to clients.

Quatrochi also noted that some investors agree with the thesis that there will be long-term content growth in the automotive space, though others have concerns about plateaus coming in 2025, as the mix from high-end vehicles normalizes after the pandemic and the mix from electric vehicles "remains slow ex-China." The sentiment for the industrial part of the market remains negative, but Quatrochi wondered if there could be a bottom in the first quarter of next year.

Trade ideas

Despite the mixed picture for the group, one popular hedge fund trade that has appeared has been to go long On Semiconductor (NASDAQ:ON) and short NXP Semiconductor (NASDAQ:NXPI), Quatrochi disclosed. Those who are bullish about On said the stock has an "undemanding" valuation and there is the opportunity for a recovery in the silicon carbide market next year, while the feedback for NXP has been "largely negative." Concerns about competition, notably from Qualcomm (QCOM), have cropped up, and there is the belief that the company's targets provided at its analyst day are "too lofty."

Other insights from investors include a "passionate" defense of Monolithic Power Systems (MPWR) after last month's sell-off, along with the "generally positive" long-term thesis about the rising benefit from electronic design automation, which should help Cadence Design Systems (CDNS) and to a lesser extent, Synopsys (SNPS). Quatrochi has an Overweight rating on Cadence and an Equal-Weight rating on Synopsys.

Lastly, sentiment is mixed on companies like Texas Instruments (TXN) and Analog Devices (ADI), while feedback was positive on Allegro Microsystems (ALGM), Quatrochi added.
